description: Edge graph connecting Tibsfox Research nodes. Phase 690 drift-hub seed (10 edges) plus Phase 707 convergent-substrate extension (7 edges). Phase 727 LeJEPA extension (10 edges).

edges.label: Drift-hub aligns with alignment audit research
edges.notes: The AAR alignment audit and drift-hub both address LLM behavioral fidelity; BCI / ASI metrics generalize the AAR concern

edges.label: Drift-hub extends the AI cluster hub
edges.notes: DRIFT is the primary v1.49.569 node in the AI & Computation cluster; connects to Erdos-1196 and Nonlinear Frontier M5 AI weather

edges.label: Drift-hub member of AI & Computation (SST) cluster
edges.notes: SST is the AI & Computation Rosetta cluster; DRIFT joins as 6th member alongside erdos-1196, GFX, BLN/M5, CSP

edges.label: Scope drift in staging layer mirrors Module D §2
edges.notes: Phase 684 CONTEXT.md D-09 Module D §2 identifies scope drift as a cross-surface phenomenon; the staging-layer research node is the engineering analog

edges.label: Module A anchors to data-science-knowledge-drift concept
edges.notes: Module A (knowledge.html) is the primary research expression of the data-science-knowledge-drift college concept

edges.label: Module B anchors to ai-computation-alignment-drift concept
edges.notes: Module B (alignment.html) is the primary research expression of the ai-computation-alignment-drift college concept

edges.label: Module C anchors to ai-computation-grounding-faithfulness concept
edges.notes: Module C (retrieval.html) is the primary research expression of the ai-computation-grounding-faithfulness college concept (SGI section)

edges.label: Drift and Erdos-1196 share AI frontier framing
edges.notes: Both DRIFT and Erdos-1196 concern the same model families (Claude 3.5, GPT-4o) in different operational modes: one drifting from instructions, the other solving prize mathematics

edges.label: Drift M5 weather models share AI-computation concern with NLF
edges.notes: NLF Module 5 (AIFS, GraphCast) are LLM-adjacent production systems; retrieval drift is a risk in live-data AI weather pipelines

edges.label: Drift hub bridges to adaptive-systems department
edges.notes: Context-equilibrium and ASI (adaptive-systems dept concepts) are direct theoretical grounding for Module B findings on multi-turn drift dynamics

edges.label: Convergent Substrate cross-validates AAR architectural audit findings
edges.notes: Phase 707 gap-closure §1 (four-tier trust) formalizes what AAR audit identified as Staging Layer weakness; the 26.1% community-skill vulnerability baseline is the beat-target

edges.label: Convergent Substrate extends the AI cluster hub
edges.notes: CONV is the primary v1.49.570 node in the AI & Computation cluster; surveys ~55 April 2026 arXiv papers mapping onto 14 gsd-skill-creator subsystems

edges.label: Convergent Substrate member of AI & Computation (SST) cluster
edges.notes: SST is the AI & Computation Rosetta cluster; CONV joins as 7th member extending the DRIFT/NLF/BLN/CSP/GFX/erdos-1196 constellation

edges.label: Convergent Substrate shares knowledge/alignment/retrieval drift vocabulary
edges.notes: CONV reuses the DRIFT taxonomy for the drift-adjacent papers in its Tier-A cluster; ECM runtime safety envelopes (Qin et al.) are directly applicable to drift defense

edges.label: Convergent Substrate Half B four-tier trust is the Staging Layer hardening candidate
edges.notes: Gap-closure candidate #1 (four-tier skill trust T1-T4) from Jiang et al. is direct Staging Layer architecture; addresses community-skill vulnerability at intake

edges.label: Capability Evolution page anchors to the ai-computation-capability-evolution college concept
edges.notes: capability-evolution.html is the primary research expression of the ECM / Amiga Principle college concept; the concept carries the relationships into four-tier-trust, harness-as-object, and two-gate-guardrail

edges.label: Compression Spectrum page anchors to the data-science-compression-spectrum college concept
edges.notes: compression-spectrum.html is the primary research expression of the Shen et al. spectrum concept; ties memory/skills/rules as one axis with the missing-diagonal gap

edges.label: LeJEPA proves the Amiga Principle in ML form
edges.notes: LeJEPA's heuristics-free stance is the mathematical form of AAR's Amiga Principle — architectural leverage replacing brute force. Load-bearing parallel (a) in the Module 4 table.

edges.label: LeJEPA extends the Convergent Substrate methodology
edges.notes: v1.49.571 follows the v1.49.570 two-half pattern (research + corpus tie-in, then default-off substrate modules). Convergent Substrate validated GSD patterns against April 2026 literature; LeJEPA adopts LeCun's distributional-objective methodology directly.

edges.label: Heuristic-free objective as drift-prevention by design
edges.notes: Drift in LLM Systems (v1.49.569) catalogued drift defenses; LeJEPA's stated distributional target is drift-prevention at the representation-space level — collapse cannot happen if isotropy is measured and enforced.

edges.label: LeJEPA anchors the AI & Computation Rosetta cluster
edges.notes: SST (Structured Sparse Theorem-proving) is the AI & Computation cluster hub; LeJEPA joins as a major member covering representation learning, world models, and heuristics-free methodology.

edges.label: LeCun's JEPA thesis: LLMs insufficient, latent prediction the path forward
edges.notes: LeCun's 14 Apr 2026 Al-Khwarizmi lecture argues autoregressive LLMs do not learn world models in the sense required for planning; JEPAs predict future latent embeddings rather than raw observations. LLM hub gets a JEPA-alternative section pointing to LEJEPA.

edges.label: Chipset allocation validated by LeJEPA's 60+ architecture result
edges.notes: LeJEPA's §6.1 architecture-zoo result (heuristics-free training works across 60+ architectures of varying capacities) independently validates the chipset specialization argument. Parallel (k) in the Module 4 table.

edges.label: In-domain > transfer validates College of Knowledge direction
edges.notes: LeJEPA's Galaxy10 result (LeJEPA pretrained in-domain beats DINOv2/v3 transfer across 1-shot to full supervision) is the existence proof that the College of Knowledge's specialized-beats-generic direction scales. Parallel (h) in the Module 4 table.

edges.label: DACP bundles as structured embeddings
edges.notes: DACP's deterministic three-part structured bundles are gsd-skill-creator's analogue of 'clean structured embeddings'; SIGReg-style isotropy checks could apply to the structured-data layer of DACP as a future strategic item (mission package T3 tier).

edges.label: Silicon Layer as natural home for SIGReg adapter
edges.notes: Silicon Layer's GPU math co-processor and LoRA pipeline is the natural home for an actual SIGReg computation primitive. Tier-3 strategic item T3b in the M5 roadmap.

edges.label: Scope drift as representation drift at the staging layer
edges.notes: Staging-layer 'scope drift' from v1.49.569 is a special case of the LeJEPA collapse problem — drift in derived content is drift in the representation of user intent. Intrinsic Telemetry (Phase 733) could correlate scope-drift signals with mission quality.
